summary:
• Community Engagement: 18 Tennessee communities, including Chattanooga, participated in Make Music Day, enhancing local cultural engagement.
  • Budget Priorities: The city budget emphasizes essential services such as police, fire, paving, and affordable housing, reflecting community investment needs.
  • Leadership Development: Dr. Jasmine Fernández joins Chattanooga 2.0 as Director of Adolescent Strategy, aiming to strengthen youth development initiatives.

items.title: Tennessee, you made some noise for Make Music Day! 🔊 Thank you to the 18 Tennessee communities that celebrated the summ...
items.content_text:
Tennessee, you made some noise for Make Music Day! 🔊
 Thank you to the 18 Tennessee communities that celebrated the summer solstice by making music together. 
 The Tennessee Entertainment Commission is honored to support initiatives that inspire creativity, showcase local talent and bring communities together across the Volunteer State. 🎶

items.title: Last year, we asked Chattanoogans to invest in the essential services that impact their lives: police, fire, paving, aff...
items.content_text:
Last year, we asked Chattanoogans to invest in the essential services that impact their lives: police, fire, paving, affordable housing, and technology to address traffic congestion. This year's budget is about providing a return on that investment. 
 The approved balanced budget hold's our property tax steady in the face of inflation without cutting services due to a citywide initiative to identify cost savings and hold the line on expenses. The tax rate remains lower than the rate residents pay in Knoxville, Nashville and Memphis. Chattanooga’s tax burden per resident is also lower than Nashville and Knoxville. Swipe to see major investments within the FY27 budget, which begins on July 1.

The tax rate remains lower than the rate residents pay in Knoxville, Nashville and Memphis. Chattanooga’s tax burden per resident is also lower than Nashville and Knoxville. Swipe to see major investments within the FY27 budget, which begins on July 1.

items.title: 👀Join the City of Chattanooga Department of Community Development Aquatics Division for a fun, family-friendly evening...
items.content_text:
👀
Join the City of Chattanooga Department of Community Development Aquatics Division for a fun, family-friendly evening at Stars, Stripes & Splashes! — The Great American Pool Party!
 Enjoy open swim, splash pad access, live DJ entertainment, patriotic games, a photo/selfie station, and complimentary light refreshments as we celebrate summer in red, white, and blue.
 ⏰📆Thursday, July 2, 2026, from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM!
 📍Warner Park Pool on Thursday, July 2, 2026, from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM!
 Admission is $2.50 and everyone is welcome!
 Get your tickets today by visiting https://cha.city/250 or by texting “250” to 423-643-7300. 
 Come ready to swim, splash, and celebrate!

items.title: Today’s Family Justice Center Strategic Summit reminded me once again how powerful a community can be when we come toget...
items.content_text:
Today’s Family Justice Center Strategic Summit reminded me once again how powerful a community can be when we come together with a shared purpose.
 We left the summit with meaningful progress and a stronger vision for promoting safety, healing, and justice for victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, child abuse, elder abuse, and human trafficking. Together, we explored ways to strengthen our coordinated response for adults and children, improve law enforcement partnerships, enhance access to Orders of Protection, engage faith communities, gather meaningful baseline data, expand healthcare and mental health services, strengthen governance, and increase community outreach and engagement. We built on the work from our planning session with Casey Gwinn in March. Casey motivated us and shined the light on best practices l. 
 Most importantly, every conversation centered on listening to and elevating the voices of survivors. Their experiences, courage, and insights must continue to guide our work.
 I am incredibly grateful for the many community leaders, professionals, advocates, and partners who invested their time and expertise today. A special thank you to Judge Christie Sell and Councilwoman Jenny Berz for their leadership and commitment to building a safer community for all.
 The solutions to violence require all of us. If you are interested in being part of this community effort and helping create a coordinated response that supports survivors and their families, we invite you to reach out to the Family Justice Center.
 Together, we can make a difference.
 #FamilyJusticeCenter #CommunityCollaboration #SurvivorCentered #EndDomesticViolence #EndSexualAssault #HopeAndHealing #Chattanooga #StrongerTogether

items.title: Please join us in welcoming Dr. Jasmine Fernández to the Chattanooga 2.0 team as our new Director of Adolescent Strategy and Colle...
items.content_text:
Please join us in welcoming Dr. Jasmine Fernández to the Chattanooga 2.0 team as our new Director of Adolescent Strategy and Collective Impact!

Dr. Fernández brings a wealth of experience in youth development, education leadership, community organizing, and student support systems. Throughout her career, she has championed efforts that expand opportunity and improve outcomes for young people, ensuring they have the resources, relationships, and guidance needed to thrive.

In this role, she will lead cross-sector efforts to strengthen outcomes for middle and high school youth across Hamilton County, helping align partners, expand access to high-quality opportunities, and support organizations serving young people throughout our community.

We are excited to welcome Dr. Fernández and look forward to the impact her leadership will have on the future of our youth.

#Chattanooga20 #HamiltonCounty #YouthSuccess #CollectiveImpact #EducationLeadership.                                     
Read more: https://lnkd.in/e2JFCdt5

items.title: 🚒 Where in Red Bank is Justin Headrick? Justin was recently spotted at Red Bank Fire Station 1 & 2, installing the new...
items.content_text:
🚒 Where in Red Bank is Justin Headrick?
 Justin was recently spotted at Red Bank Fire Station 1 & 2, installing the new Air Vac 911 exhaust removal systems.
 These systems are 100% automatic — they activate the moment a fire apparatus drives through the bay door. The result: cleaner air in the apparatus bay and better protection for our firefighters.
 Proud to see important upgrades happening right here in our community.

items.title: New mural to transform 11th Street entrance to MLK District across from City Hall
items.content_text: Argentine American Artist Cecilia Lueza Selected by Community Panel for Vibrant and Uplifting WorkCHATTANOOGA, Tenn. (June 22, 2026) – The MLK Art in Neighborhoods project at the City Hall Annex will provide a transformative and vibrant entry to the 11th Street corridor of the MLK District. The new mural will reflect elements of Chattanooga’s rolling hills, the Tennessee River, and beloved backyard birds, serving as a welcoming gateway to the City of Chattanooga’s central hub and the MLK neighborhood. The MLK Art in Neighborhoods project at the City Hall Annex will provide a transformative and vibrant entry to the 11th Street corridor of the MLK District. items.title: Chattanooga Convention Center Posted
HCSO Utilizes Drone Technology to Locate Stolen Vehicle Hamilton County, TN – On Friday, June 19, 2026, an HCSO deputy r...
items.content_text:
HCSO Utilizes Drone Technology to Locate Stolen Vehicle
 Hamilton County, TN – On Friday, June 19, 2026, an HCSO deputy responded to the scene of a stolen tractor, valued at approximately $15,000, to help locate the vehicle. 
 An HCSO drone was launched to canvass the area, allowing personnel to both efficiently locate the tractor and ensure the safety of our deputies by checking for potential threats and hazards.
 This is one of many ways the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office has utilized drone technology since 2016, and this resource continues to demonstrate how effective and essential it is during law enforcement operations.
items.content_html:
HCSO Utilizes Drone Technology to Locate Stolen Vehicle
Hamilton County, TN – On Friday, June 19, 2026, an HCSO deputy responded to the scene of a stolen tractor, valued at approximately $15,000, to help locate the vehicle.
An HCSO drone was launched to canvass the area, allowing personnel to both efficiently locate the tractor and ensure the safety of our deputies by checking for potential threats and hazards.
This is one of many ways the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office has utilized drone technology since 2016, and this resource continues to demonstrate how effective and essential it is during law enforcement operations.

items.title: COME FROM AWAY AUDITIONS Join us for one of the most inspiring true stories ever brought to the stage as we open our 103...
items.content_text:
COME FROM AWAY AUDITIONS
 Join us for one of the most inspiring true stories ever brought to the stage as we open our 103rd season with Come From Away!
 Sign-up here: https://www.theatrecentre.com/auditions
 ***You will receive an email confirmation with an audition form to fill out!***
 AUDITION DATES:
 July 6 & 7, 2026
 *Note: Please sign up for one audition date only. 
 ABOUT THE SHOW: Come From Away tells the true story of Gander, Newfoundland, where 38 planes and thousands of stranded travelers were diverted on September 11, 2001. Over a few extraordinary days, the people of this small town welcomed the world with open arms, music, and a reminder that we are all part of one global family. 
 CREATIVE TEAM:
 Director: Blake Harris
 Music Director: John Thomas McCecil
 Choreographer: Ashley Tisdale
 Stage Manager: Jill Burgess
 Assistant Stage Manager: Amanda Legge
 LOCATION:
 Chattanooga Theatre Centre - Circle Lobby
 *Note: A parking pass will be provided upon arrival and should be displayed on your vehicle's dashboard.
 WHAT TO PREPARE:
 You will be taught all the necessary music and movement combinations on the night of your audition. Please dress to move: Wear comfortable clothing that allows for full range of motion.
 Footwear: You are welcome to bring dance shoes for the choreography portion, though they are not required. Please avoid open-toed shoes or non-dance heels.
 ROLES + CASTING INFORMATION:
 ROLES AVAILABLE: 12–15 adult actors (ages 18+) will be cast.
 AGES: Most roles call for actors who appear 40 or older, with a few roles for actors who read as being in their 20s and 30s.
 RACE & ETHNICITY: Come From Away brings the whole world to one small town, and we want our cast to reflect that. All heritages and ethnicities are celebrated in this production. We especially encourage performers from MENA (Middle Eastern and North African), Black, Latine, Indigenous, and Asian communities to audition.
 DANCE & PHYSICAL ABILITIES: Choreography for this production will celebrate each performer's individual range of motion and physical expression. We welcome people of all physical abilities, and we encourage you not to let prior dance experience - or preconceptions about it - keep you from auditioning.
 MULTIPLE ROLES: Each actor will play several characters, often shifting between distinct accents and dialects throughout the show.
 PRIOR EXPERIENCE: Prior experience is not required. We encourage everyone with an interest, passion, and curiosity to audition!
 CALLBACKS: Thursday, July 9, 2026, at 7:00 PM
 REHEARSAL SCHEDULE: Rehearsals typically take place Sunday through Friday from 7:00 PM to 10:00 PM. *Note: You may not be called for every rehearsal.
 PERFORMANCE DATES: September 11th-13th, 17th-20th, & 24th-27th.
 CONFLICTS:
 Please review your calendar. You must be prepared to list all conflicts within the rehearsal timeframe (including late arrivals or early departures). Please note that attendance is mandatory for tech week and all performances; no conflicts will be permitted during these dates.

items.title: Dr. Elizabeth O’Brien, UTC vice provost for faculty affairs, has been selected for the 2026-27 Becoming a Provost Academ...
items.content_text:
Dr. Elizabeth O’Brien, UTC vice provost for faculty affairs, has been selected for the 2026-27 Becoming a Provost Academy, a yearlong leadership program of the American Association of State Colleges and Universities (AASCU) designed to prepare aspiring chief academic officers. 👏 
 “This is an opportunity to learn and extend my knowledge of the provost role,” O’Brien said. “It gives me the opportunity to have some perspective on what we do really well here at UTC, but also the places where I can grow and develop my skills even further.”
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/utcs-elizabeth-obrien-chosen-for-na…/

items.title: Did you know that the new Trump Accounts are a savings option for kids under 18? For children born between January 1, 20...
items.content_text: Did you know that the new Trump Accounts are a savings option for kids under 18? For children born between January 1, 2025, and December 31, 2028, the federal government will automatically make a one-time contribution of $1000 to their account! 🎉💰 This is a great opportunity to help our children start their financial journey on the right foot! Let's make sure they know about this amazing benefit! #FutureInvestments #ChildSavings #TrumpAccounts

items.title: Fun fact: Hamilton County was officially established on October 25, 1819! From the region’s earliest settlements to the...
items.content_text:
Fun fact: Hamilton County was officially established on October 25, 1819!
 From the region’s earliest settlements to the growth of Chattanooga, our history has shaped just what Hamilton County is today. Join us at the United250 to celebrate what makes not only our country, but our county, so special!
 🗓️ Sat, July 4, 2026
 📍 Ross's Landing - Chattanooga, TN
 💲 Free Event

items.title: 🚨 KEEP FIRE HYDRANTS CLEAR – HELP US SAVE LIVES AND PROPERTY! 🚨 In an emergency, every single second counts. When fire...
items.content_text:
🚨 KEEP FIRE HYDRANTS CLEAR – HELP US SAVE LIVES AND PROPERTY! 🚨
 In an emergency, every single second counts. When fire hydrants are blocked or obscured, the vital time we need to secure a water source is lost. That delay can put your home and your life at risk. A clear hydrant gives us the access we need to work fast and protect our community.
items.content_html:

items.title: Join the Office of Arts, Culture and Creative Economy for a weekend celebrating creativity, conversation, and community....
items.content_text:
Join the Office of Arts, Culture and Creative Economy for a weekend celebrating creativity, conversation, and community.
 Saturday, June 27 | Culture on 4
 Grammy-nominated producer, songwriter, and musician Avery Johnson joins Eric Foster for an intimate conversation about music, creativity, and a career spanning more than 30 years. From East Tennessee to working with some of the biggest names in the industry, Avery’s story offers inspiration and insight for creatives and music lovers alike.
 📍 Chattanooga Public Library, 4th Floor
 🕕 6:00 PM
 🎟 Free and open to the public
 Sunday, June 28 | Democracy Speaks: Opening Celebration
 In partnership with Rhyme N Chatt, we invite you to experience the next phase of Poetry Pathways through an evening of poetry, spoken word, live music, food trucks, and community gathering. Democracy Speaks explores themes of freedom, justice, democracy, and community through the power of creative expression.
 📍 Renaissance Park
 🕠 5:30 PM – 8:30 PM
 🎟 Free and open to the public
 Whether you’re joining us for music, poetry, conversation, or community, we hope you’ll spend part of your weekend with us.
 #CultureOn4 #DemocracySpeaks #PoetryPathways #ChattanoogaArts #CreativeChattanooga ArtsAndCulture

items.title: Our Election Registration event is almost here!🗳️ This Saturday, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM, we will be helping...
items.content_text:
Our Election Registration event is almost here!🗳️
 This Saturday, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM, we will be helping community members register for the upcoming local elections on August 6. There will be FREE ice cream, kids' activities, community resources and much more, so don't miss it!💚 You can also stop by Café del Sur in the morning to receive useful information about voter registration and sweet bread.☕️
 This event is organized by United by Change Coalition - Hamilton County, TN, CALEB - Chattanooga in Action for Love, Equality and Benevolence and La Paz Chattanooga.

items.title: Today, we're continuing our Exploring Downtown series with Main Terrain Art Park. Tucked into Chattanooga’s Southside, M...
items.content_text:
Today, we're continuing our Exploring Downtown series with Main Terrain Art Park.
 Tucked into Chattanooga’s Southside, Main Terrain Art Park is a creative example of how art, recreation, and sustainability can come together to build an active public space. What was once a vacant, underutilized lot at the end of Main Street was transformed into a 1.72-acre public art and fitness park through a partnership between the City of Chattanooga, local organizations, and support from the National Endowment for the Arts. The park’s signature sculptural elements, designed by artist Thomas Sayre, reflect the architecture of the Walnut Street Bridge. 
 As you explore the park, you’ll find interactive art, fitness features, walking paths, and stormwater management systems woven into the landscape. Next time you're in Downtown Chattanooga, take a walk through this unique landmark!

items.title: There's still time to sign up for our Tai Chi classes at the Chris Ramsey Sr. Center and the East Lake Center! Check the...
items.content_text:
There's still time to sign up for our Tai Chi classes at the Chris Ramsey Sr. Center and the East Lake Center! Check the comments for sign up!
Looking for a way to improve your strength, balance, and overall well-being? Join us for our FREE Tai Chi for Fall Prevention and Arthritis classes at the East Lake Community Center in July and the Chris Ramsey Sr. Community Center in September! 
 Led by certified instructors, these gentle, evidence-based classes can help reduce arthritis symptoms, improve balance, and lower the risk of falls. The program is designed for seniors, but adults of all fitness levels can benefit from this relaxing form of exercise. Space is limited, and registration is required. CC for details
 https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx…

items.title: How big are the new wastewater storage tanks? Come with us as we take an exclusive look inside…  Checking back in on the...
items.content_text:
How big are the new wastewater storage tanks? Come with us as we take an exclusive look inside… 
 Checking back in on the e2i2 Equalization Station off of I-75, the equalization tanks are almost ready for use. The West Chickamauga EQ Site features two 15 million gallon tanks for storing excess flow in the sanitary sewer system during heavy rainfall events. This additional storage capacity will prevent sanitary sewer overflows (SSOs) into South Chickamauga Creek and the Tennessee River. 
 Thank you to the Chattanooga Wastewater Department for the hard work toward this public safety initiative!

items.title: Early detection saves lives. The Hamilton County Health Department provides Women’s Health Services to help you prioriti...
items.content_text:
Early detection saves lives.
 The Hamilton County Health Department provides Women’s Health Services to help you prioritize your health. Taking care of yourself is essential not only for you but also for those who care about you.
 Our services include, but are not limited to, well-woman visits, vaccinations, and age-appropriate exams.
 All of our locations offer Women’s Health Services. To find the location that works best for you, please visit http://health.hamiltontn.org/…/sexualreproductivehealthclin… or call (423) 209-8009.

items.title: Strike one. Strike two. Strike three... BOOTED. Starting October 1st, vehicles with 3+ outstanding parking tickets 60 da...
items.content_text:
Strike one. Strike two. Strike three... BOOTED.
 Starting October 1st, vehicles with 3+ outstanding parking tickets 60 days or older are eligible for booting.
 Good news: you've still got time to step up to the plate. Pay your outstanding tickets by June 30th and get 35% OFF your total balance.
 Don't let this be a swing and a miss.
 Visit www.chattanoogaparking.org to pay your outstanding tickets.

items.title: What's better than butter & baguettes? 🧈 This Friday night, join Scenic City Clay Arts and The Chattery for a creative...
items.content_text:
What's better than butter & baguettes? 🧈
 This Friday night, join Scenic City Clay Arts and The Chattery for a creative pottery class featuring a fresh baguette from Oui Oui Boulangerie. 
 In this class, you'll handmake a ceramic butter dish designed to elevate everyday meals. SCCA's instructor Summer Tomes will guide you through each step, from shaping and carving to adding unique surface textures and decorative details. 
 While you won't get to take home your butter dish immediately after class (it'll be ready in 6 - 8 weeks), you will get to take home a delicious French baguette. 🥖
 ❗️Ticket sales end on 6/24 (and they're going fast).❗️Sign up here: https://thechattery.org/…/butter-baguettes-make-your-own-ce….

items.title: The Chattanooga Public Library is just 1 week away!📚 ​​Visit our offices on Tuesday, June 30...
items.content_text:
We're only 1 week away from visiting The Chattanooga Public Library!📚
 Visit our offices on Tuesday, June 30, from 10 AM to 12 PM, to register for a library card and learn about all the free services they offer!🥳
 @followers
Learn about the Chattanooga Public Library at this FREE event!📚
 Poppy, The Chattanooga Public Library's bookmobile, will be giving us a fun tour on Tuesday, June 30, from 10 AM to 12 PM. 🌞 
 At this event you can learn how to register to obtain a public library card, and obtain the possibility of renting books, utensils, participating in activities and crafts, much more and all at NO COST.🤩
 The event will be at the La Paz offices, located at 809 S Willow St, Chattanooga, TN. There is no registration required, everyone is welcome to come with their families!

items.title: Congratulations to Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on their recent ribbon cutting! 🎀✂️ Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on Market Street is...
items.content_text:
Congratulations to Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on their recent ribbon cutting! 🎀✂️ 

Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe on Market Street is a locally owned restaurant serving fresh, Mediterranean-inspired meals in the heart of downtown Chattanooga. Known for its grilled meats, fresh salads, gyros, sandwiches, and signature dips, Taziki's has been a part of the downtown community since 2011 and continues to be a favorite spot for lunch, dinner, catering, and gathering with friends and family.  

📍 432 Market St.
🔗 https://lnkd.in/eTtvH-nr

items.title: Thank you, Dallos Vinyl Love, for your generous donation to the Urban League of Greater Chattanooga. We were excited to...
items.content_text:
Thank you, Dallos Vinyl Love, for your generous donation to the Urban League of Greater Chattanooga. We were excited to see our President & CEO, Dr. Reginald Smith II, showcasing his skills as one of your many Vibe Masters during your first anniversary celebration. 
 We are extremely grateful to companies like yours for investing in our organization, allowing us to continue transforming lives and empowering our community.
 If you would like to support the Urban League of Greater Chattanooga, you can make a donation by clicking the link in our bio. We truly appreciate your support!
